Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course covers "Gesture and language disorders," which relates to understanding communication impairments and promoting well-being, linking to Target 3.4.

Quality Education.reason: As tertiary education, it covers "first language development," "second language learning," and "language disorders," supporting inclusive and effective learning (Targets 4.3, 4.5, 4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops "communication and cooperation" and "presentation skills," which are valuable for productive employment, though this is an indirect outcome (Target 8.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Promotes social inclusion by studying "Gesture and language disorders" (disability) and fostering understanding of diverse "cultural connotations," aligning with Target 10.2.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: By fostering "open and diverse communication concepts," the course indirectly supports intercultural understanding, which is a foundation for peaceful societies (related to Target 16.b).
